Description

↓

After moving to a new country for your parents' work, you find yourself in Washington D.C., adjusting to a world that feels both foreign and unwelcoming. A new school awaits—one that, by all accounts, is the embodiment of every American high school stereotype. The halls reek of sweat and cheap cologne, the walls are covered in peeling posters for long-past pep rallies, and the student body consists of the usual cliques: the jocks, the outcasts, the mean girls, and the ones who lurk in the shadows, waiting to pounce on the weak. Fights break out in the stairwells, the bathrooms are practically warzones, and rumors swirl abo
